mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, tighter policy raises discount rates and reduces equity duration value; the crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5% after being at or below 1.5% in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals weakening labor income and recession risk; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns following the trigger are not lower than unconditional returns, or if the trigger has a nonnegative hit-rate-adjusted information coefficient over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with rising policy rates marks late-cycle overheating; when UNRATE is below 4% and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months, valuation pressure predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and FEDFUNDS increases over the prior 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not underperform cases with only one condition present, or if triggered 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production larger than 0.5% over 3 months signals weaker goods output and earnings pressure; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 5-day SPX returns are not below unconditional 5-day returns, or if the signal's downside hit rate is below 50% over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts during rising policy rates signals rate-sensitive demand destruction; when HOUST declines over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls over 3 months and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either HOUST decline alone or FEDFUNDS rise alone, or if Rank IC is nonnegative over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI during a low-rate setting signals negative real-rate pressure and future tightening risk; when CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.6% over 3 months with FEDFUNDS below 2%, 20-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.6% over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%
regime_hint: inflation acceleration before full policy adjustment
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-low-rate trigger produces nonnegative average 20-day SPX excess returns versus baseline, or if triggered returns are positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth over two consecutive quarters signals broad demand expansion and stronger aggregate earnings; higher GDPC1 momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive two-quarter GDPC1 momentum fails to produce higher 60-day SPX returns than negative momentum, or if the rolling 5-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.
